excludedSurfaces.app-partner: The partner consumes order preparation after checkout and does not own client cart truth.
excludedSurfaces.app-captain: Captain execution begins after order creation and assignment.
excludedSurfaces.app-field: Field operations do not mutate client carts.
excludedSurfaces.wlt-ui: WLT owns payment and ledger truth but no pre-checkout cart mutation.

capabilities: The client loads one persisted DSH cart and reads committed items after every mutation.
Add, quantity update, remove, and explicit clear are actor-owned and server-authorized.
Product, store assortment, name, and unit price are snapshotted only from current DSH catalog truth.
Delivery serviceability resolves an address owned by the authenticated client and evaluates store, zone, requested mode, capacity, and SLA.
Cart reads reconcile each immutable snapshot against current price, availability, product linkage, and assortment identity.
A client cannot create a second active cart for another store; explicit clear abandons the old cart.
The control panel reads the same persisted items and reconciliation state without financial mutation.
Runtime evidence persists every address-owned serviceability check without storing payment or ledger truth.
negativeInvariants: No client-supplied price, product name, assortment identity, service-area code, latitude, or longitude establishes cart truth.
No cart identifier permits mutation without authenticated client ownership.
No client has more than one active cart across stores.
No silent store switch destroys an active cart.
No price or assortment change silently rewrites an existing snapshot.
No unavailable, unpriced, unlinked, or changed item is reported checkout-ready.
No exhausted or throttled capacity is reported serviceable.
No control-panel cart view writes payment, settlement, refund, or ledger state.
No runtime mock or local cart establishes operational truth.
technicalAcceptanceConditions: All eight entries in the JRN-009 slice registry are closed by implementation and same-commit evidence.
Cart and mounted HTTP Go tests pass.
PostgreSQL applies every DSH migration including dsh-096.
Runtime proves owned address, serviceability, capacity, SLA, snapshot reconciliation, store isolation, operator readback, delete, and clear.
